extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won the Champs-Élysées sprint after surviving the mountains and recognizing his final career opportunity on cobblestones.
tactical_patterns: LOMPART Steve's late breakaway with OSSA Daniel nearly stole sprint thunder, caught only 200 meters from line.
KRISTOFF hit 40mph on cobblestone drag uphill, superior traction/power transfer gave decisive edge over DEGENKOLB.
Faster sprinters absent after mountain attrition created opportunity window that KRISTOFF's team positioned perfectly to exploit.

rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: First win since 2014 after five top-fives this Tour; superior power delivery on technical cobblestone finish.
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: positive: Went shoulder-to-shoulder with winner on bumpy surface, nearly equaled KRISTOFF's speed despite technical disadvantage.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Rode defensively in Stage 20 TT after second time check, freewheeling downhills when lead became insurmountable.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Nearly won Stage 20 TT after being written off; secured podium when most expected him outside top-3.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: High-speed crash days before Paris eliminated likely stage win; absence from final sprint reduced field quality.
